subject: Computer Networking
title: DMARC — Policy Enforcement
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on DMARC — Policy Enforcement.

hook.text: SPF and DKIM detect fakes. DMARC tells the receiver what to do about them: nothing, quarantine, or reject.

buildup.text: DMARC — Domain-based Message Authentication — links SPF and DKIM results to the visible From address.

discovery.text: A DMARC DNS record sets policy: p=none (monitor), p=quarantine (spam folder), or p=reject (block).

twist.text: DMARC sends reports back to domain owners showing who is sending email using their domain — legit or not.

climax.text: Google and Yahoo now require DMARC for bulk senders. No DMARC, no inbox.

punchline.text: DMARC enforces the rules SPF and DKIM set. Action, not just detection.

quiz.question: What does a DMARC policy of p=reject do?
options: Tells the receiver to block emails that fail authentication
Sends a warning to the sender
Logs the email but delivers it normally
